nl_question: What diseases are associated with CFTR?

verification_guide.summary: 3 diseases verified against the official NCBI Gene Phenotypes table for CFTR (gene/1080). Each entry is one row of that table, with cross-references to OMIM, MedGen, and (where applicable) GeneReviews. Each is also returned by PloverDB for the gold query.
sources_used: NCBI Gene record for CFTR — Phenotypes section. URL: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gene/1080 — anchor: #header-phenotypes
verification_guide.how_to_verify_an_entry: Open the URL https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gene/1080. Use the in-page TOC or scroll to the section header titled "Phenotypes" (an H2; anchor id=header-phenotypes). Immediately below the heading is a table with columns [Description, Tests], alphabetical by disease name. Each verified_answer corresponds to one row of that table — match by the Description column. The exact_quote field reproduces the row's Description + cross-refs verbatim.
verification_guide.cross_check_with_ploverdb: Each verified_answer's CURIE (MONDO:0009061, MONDO:0010178, MONDO:0008887) was confirmed present in the PloverDB result set for the gold one-hop query (n0=NCBIGene:1080, n1=biolink:Disease, e0=biolink:gene_associated_with_condition). PloverDB returned 561 disease CURIEs total; the 3 here are the canonical CFTR-associated diseases per NCBI's authoritative gene-phenotype curation.
verification_guide.intentionally_omitted: The NCBI Phenotypes table also lists Hereditary pancreatitis (OMIM:167800), but its GeneReviews link points to "PRSS1-Related Hereditary Pancreatitis" — PRSS1 is the primary causal gene, CFTR is a modifier. Excluded from the gold set to keep gene_associated_with_condition strict (CFTR-primary only).

evidence.page_title: CFTR — CF transmembrane conductance regulator [Homo sapiens (human)] — NCBI Gene
evidence.publisher: U.S. National Center for Biotechnology Information — NCBI Gene
evidence.where_on_page: On the CFTR gene page, scroll to (or click) the section header titled "Phenotypes" (H2). Directly below it is a table with columns [Description, Tests]. Look for the row whose Description column starts with the exact text "Cystic fibrosis" — it shows MedGen: C0010674, OMIM: 219700, GeneReviews: Cystic Fibrosis.
evidence.exact_quote: Cystic fibrosis  MedGen: C0010674  OMIM: 219700  GeneReviews: Cystic Fibrosis

verified_answers.label: congenital bilateral aplasia of vas deferens from CFTR mutation

evidence.page_title: CFTR — CF transmembrane conductance regulator [Homo sapiens (human)] — NCBI Gene
evidence.publisher: U.S. National Center for Biotechnology Information — NCBI Gene
evidence.where_on_page: On the CFTR gene page, scroll to the "Phenotypes" section (H2). In the table with columns [Description, Tests], find the row whose Description starts with "Congenital bilateral aplasia of vas deferens from CFTR mutation" — note the disease name itself contains "from CFTR mutation". The row shows MedGen: C0403814, OMIM: 277180, GeneReviews: Cystic Fibrosis.
evidence.exact_quote: Congenital bilateral aplasia of vas deferens from CFTR mutation  MedGen: C0403814  OMIM: 277180  GeneReviews: Cystic Fibrosis

verified_answers.label: bronchiectasis with or without elevated sweat chloride 1

evidence.page_title: CFTR — CF transmembrane conductance regulator [Homo sapiens (human)] — NCBI Gene
evidence.publisher: U.S. National Center for Biotechnology Information — NCBI Gene
evidence.where_on_page: On the CFTR gene page, scroll to the "Phenotypes" section (H2). In the table with columns [Description, Tests], find the row whose Description starts with "Bronchiectasis with or without elevated sweat chloride 1" — usually the FIRST row of the table (it's alphabetical and starts with B). The row shows MedGen: C2749757, OMIM: 211400.
evidence.exact_quote: Bronchiectasis with or without elevated sweat chloride 1  MedGen: C2749757  OMIM: 211400
